content:
- marked story-19 task 03 done with <passes>true</passes>, ran .ralph/task_switch.sh, committed all changes in e7d4c24, and pushed master to origin
- no AGENTS.md update was needed; the behavior was captured by code/tests/task artifacts without adding repo-level instruction debt
- should do next: stop here

- refreshed the Ralph task summary, read the choose-task rules, previous progress, active tasks, done tasks, and the active task files to resolve blockers and ordering
- confirmed there are no unblocked failing bug tasks; among failing non-bug tasks only story-22a task 02 is explicitly blocked, and the Rust workspace/codebase already shows the runner scratch-image contract exists while downstream workflow publication is still incomplete
- chose .ralph/tasks/story-20-runner-scratch-image/01-task-build-the-runner-as-a-scratch-image-with-one-binary-that-applies-webhook-requests-to-postgresql.md as the next task, wrote it to .ralph/current_task.txt, and should do next: execute that task with the tdd skill, validate make check/make test/make lint, then revisit story-20 task 02 or story-21 based on resulting gaps

- read saved progress, current task pointer, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill files
- confirmed the active task is story-20 task 01 for building the runner as a scratch image with a single binary applying webhook requests to PostgreSQL
- should do next: inspect the task markdown and its planning state, then either create/update the plan or begin execution depending on whether it ends in TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE

- read the active task markdown and confirmed it does not yet include a plan/execution marker
- determined this turn must create a separate plan file and stop, per the Ralph workflow instructions
- should do next: inspect existing *_plans conventions and the current runner/scratch/webhook code paths to draft a TDD execution plan with a concrete boundary-cleanup target

- inspected representative task/plan files and confirmed the repo convention: task file gets a <plan> path and the plan file ends with TO BE VERIFIED or NOW EXECUTE
- inspected the runner CLI, Dockerfile, workflow, webhook runtime, runtime plan, persistence layer, and current runner contracts; the scratch single-binary runtime contract already exists, so the plan must target the remaining behavioral/test gaps and one concrete boundary cleanup rather than a broad rewrite
- should do next: compare task 01 with the follow-on story-20 task 02, then write a narrow but bold execution plan and update the task file with the plan path

- created the story-20 task-01 plan file and added a <plan> pointer to the task markdown
- the plan is marked NOW EXECUTE because the current repo state is clear enough: scratch single-binary runner already exists, the main gaps are default-lane image artifact proof and a typed cleanup of the webhook routing table boundary
- should do next: on the next turn, read the plan path from the task file, execute the TDD slices in order, run make check/make lint/make test, and only touch make test-long if the long-lane contract itself changes

- read saved progress, current task path, and the required tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill files
- confirmed prior turn already created a plan and likely left the task in NOW EXECUTE state, so the next step is to read the task markdown and linked plan and then execute the TDD slices
- should do next: inspect the task file for its <plan> pointer and execution markers, then follow the plan exactly unless the design is clearly wrong

- read the active task markdown and linked execution plan; the plan is explicitly NOW EXECUTE
- extracted the two concrete workstreams from the plan: default-lane artifact tests for the scratch runner image and typed cleanup of webhook source-table routing
- should do next: inspect the current runner files and working tree, then execute the first red/green slice against the existing implementation rather than redesigning further

- started the first RED slice by adding crates/runner/tests/image_contract.rs with default-lane artifact tests for build, entrypoint, help surface, and exported runtime filesystem
- expected result is a failing compile/test target because the runner-side artifact harness and minimal-filesystem assertion do not exist yet
- should do next: run the targeted image-contract test to capture the red failure, then implement the smallest runner artifact harness and docker contract support to turn it green

- implemented the runner-side artifact harness and a shared runtime-filesystem assertion in RunnerDockerContract
- this mirrors the verify image contract shape but keeps the runner image build rooted at the repo Dockerfile, which is the actual public artifact boundary for this task
- should do next: run the new image_contract target, inspect any failing runtime-filesystem or help-surface assumptions, and then move to the typed webhook routing slice

- started the routing-boundary RED slice by adding a public webhook_contract test that posts a quoted table identifier through runner run and expects it to land in the configured helper table
- this is the concrete boundary failure for the current stringly schema.table lookup: config identifiers are normalized, but webhook payload identifiers are not
- should do next: run the targeted quoted-identifier webhook test, then change payload/runtime-plan/routing to carry a typed QualifiedTableName key end-to-end

- replaced the stringly routing boundary with a typed QualifiedTableName flowing from webhook payload parse into MappingRuntimePlan helper-table lookup and reconcile table lookup
- the cleanup deletes SourceMetadata string rendering from the routing path and leaves table.label() only at error boundaries, which is exactly the improve-code-boundaries target from the plan
- should do next: rerun the quoted-identifier webhook test and the new image_contract suite, then move to repo-wide make check/make lint/make test

- both focused slices are green: the new runner image artifact contract tests pass, and the quoted-identifier webhook contract test passes after replacing the stringly routing key with QualifiedTableName
- no design reversal was needed; the plan remains valid and the work is now at the repo-wide verification stage
- should do next: run make check, then make lint, then make test, fixing only the first failing lane at a time and updating task status only after all required lanes pass

- make test failed in e2e_integrity_contract on the raw-docker allowlist, not on runtime behavior: the new runner_image_artifact_harness issues docker commands and must be admitted explicitly by the integrity boundary
- this is a real contract update, not a test skip; the suite is correctly forcing the repo to acknowledge every file allowed to orchestrate docker directly
- should do next: update the e2e integrity support allowlist to include the new runner image artifact harness, rerun the failing make test lane, then finish task metadata and git steps only after all required lanes are green

- all required gates for story-20 task 01 are green: make check, make lint, and make test passed without needing make test-long
- updated the task markdown to status done with <passes>true</passes> and checked the applicable acceptance criteria; no AGENTS.md update is needed because the new constraints already live in code/tests
- should do next: run task_switch.sh, commit every tracked change including .ralph artifacts with a task-finished message, push, and stop immediately
